A. 254
B. 213
C. 142
D. 71

sourceQuestion:
Consider a sequence of elements a_0, a_1, ..., a_9 where a_i = 2i+1 for 0 <= i <= 9. The following operations are performed on a stack S and a queue Q, both of which are initially empty.

I.   Push the elements a_0 to a_4 in that order into S.
II.  Enqueue the elements a_5 to a_9 in that order into Q.
III. Pop an element from S.
IV.  Dequeue an element from Q.
V.   Pop an element from S.
VI.  Dequeue an element from Q.
VII. Pop an element from Q and push the same element into S.
VIII. Repeat operation VII three times.

The top element of S after executing all the above operations is ___________.

A. a_5
B. a_8
C. a_6
D. a_9

parsed.stem:
Consider a sequence of elements a_0, a_1, ..., a_9 where a_i = 2i+1 for 0 <= i <= 9. The following operations are performed on a stack S and a queue Q, both of which are initially empty.

I.   Push the elements a_0 to a_4 in that order into S.
II.  Enqueue the elements a_5 to a_9 in that order into Q.
III. Pop an element from S.
IV.  Dequeue an element from Q.
V.   Pop an element from S.
VI.  Dequeue an element from Q.
VII. Pop an element from Q and push the same element into S.
VIII. Repeat operation VII three times.

The top element of S after executing all the above operations is ___________.

sourceQuestion:
Let S1 and S2 be two stacks. S1 has capacity of 4 elements. S2 has capacity of 2 elements. S1 already has 4 elements: 100, 200, 300, and 400 (where 400 is the top element), whereas S2 is empty, as shown below.

Stack S1 (top to bottom): 400, 300, 200, 100
Stack S2: empty

Only the following three operations are available:
- PushToS2: Pop the top element from S1 and push it on S2.
- PopToS1: Pop the top element from S2 and push it on S1.
- GenerateOutput: Pop the top element from S1 and output it to the user.

Note that the pop operation is not allowed on an empty stack and the push operation is not allowed on a full stack.

Which of the following output sequences can be generated by using the above operations?
A. 100, 200, 400, 300
B. 200, 300, 400, 100
C. 400, 200, 300, 100
D. 100, 200, 300, 400

parsed.stem:
Let S1 and S2 be two stacks. S1 has capacity of 4 elements. S2 has capacity of 2 elements. S1 already has 4 elements: 100, 200, 300, and 400 (where 400 is the top element), whereas S2 is empty, as shown below.

Stack S1 (top to bottom): 400, 300, 200, 100
Stack S2: empty

Only the following three operations are available:
- PushToS2: Pop the top element from S1 and push it on S2.
- PopToS1: Pop the top element from S2 and push it on S1.
- GenerateOutput: Pop the top element from S1 and output it to the user.

Note that the pop operation is not allowed on an empty stack and the push operation is not allowed on a full stack.

Which of the following output sequences can be generated by using the above operations?

sourceQuestion:
Consider a stack data structure into which we can PUSH and POP records. Assume that each record pushed in the stack has a positive integer key and that all keys are distinct. We wish to augment the stack data structure with an O(1) time MIN operation that returns a pointer to the record with the smallest key present in the stack:
1. without deleting the corresponding record, and
2. without changing the complexities of the standard stack operations.

Which one or more of the following approach(es) can achieve it?
A. Keep a pointer below each record in the stack, a pointer to the record with the smallest key below it in the stack.
B. Keep a pointer to the record with the smallest key in the stack.
C. Keep the key values of the records in the stack are maintained in sorted order.
D. Keep a Min Heap in which the key values of the records in the stack are maintained.
sourceAnswer: A. Keep a pointer below each record in the stack, a pointer to the record with the smallest key below it in the stack.

parsed.stem:
Consider a stack data structure into which we can PUSH and POP records. Assume that each record pushed in the stack has a positive integer key and that all keys are distinct. We wish to augment the stack data structure with an O(1) time MIN operation that returns a pointer to the record with the smallest key present in the stack:
1. without deleting the corresponding record, and
2. without changing the complexities of the standard stack operations.

Which one or more of the following approach(es) can achieve it?

options.text: Keep a pointer below each record in the stack, a pointer to the record with the smallest key below it in the stack.
Keep a pointer to the record with the smallest key in the stack.
Keep the key values of the records in the stack are maintained in sorted order.
Keep a Min Heap in which the key values of the records in the stack are maintained.

sourceQuestion:
A program attempts to generate as many permutations as possible of the string 'abcd' by pushing the characters a, b, c, d in the same order onto a stack, but it may pop off the top character at any time. Which one of the following strings CANNOT be generated using this program?

A. abcd
B. dcba
C. cbad
D. cabd

parsed.stem: A program attempts to generate as many permutations as possible of the string 'abcd' by pushing the characters a, b, c, d in the same order onto a stack, but it may pop off the top character at any time. Which one of the following strings CANNOT be generated using this program?

sourceQuestion:
A function f defined on stacks of integers satisfies the following properties:
f(empty_stack) = 0
f(push(S, i)) = max(f(S), 0) + i, for all stacks S and integers i.

If a stack S contains the integers 2, -3, 2, -1, 2 in order from bottom to top, what is f(S)?

A. 6
B. 4
C. 3
D. 2

parsed.stem:
A function f defined on stacks of integers satisfies the following properties:
f(empty_stack) = 0
f(push(S, i)) = max(f(S), 0) + i, for all stacks S and integers i.

If a stack S contains the integers 2, -3, 2, -1, 2 in order from bottom to top, what is f(S)?

sourceQuestion:
A meld operation on two instances of a data structure combines them into one single instance of the same data structure. Consider the following data structures:

P. Unsorted doubly linked list with pointers to the head node and tail node of the list.
Q. Min-heap implemented using an array.
R. Binary Search Tree.

Which ONE of the following options gives the worst-case time complexities for the meld operation on instances of size n of the above data structures?

A. P: Theta(1),    Q: Theta(n),      R: Theta(n)
B. P: Theta(n),    Q: Theta(n log n), R: Theta(n)
C. P: Theta(n log n), Q: Theta(n log n), R: Theta(n^2)
D. P: Theta(1),    Q: Theta(n log n), R: Theta(n log n)
sourceAnswer: D. P: Theta(1), Q: Theta(n log n), R: Theta(n log n)

parsed.stem:
A meld operation on two instances of a data structure combines them into one single instance of the same data structure. Consider the following data structures:

P. Unsorted doubly linked list with pointers to the head node and tail node of the list.
Q. Min-heap implemented using an array.
R. Binary Search Tree.

Which ONE of the following options gives the worst-case time complexities for the meld operation on instances of size n of the above data structures?

options.text: P: Theta(1), Q: Theta(n), R: Theta(n)
P: Theta(n), Q: Theta(n log n), R: Theta(n)
P: Theta(n log n), Q: Theta(n log n), R: Theta(n^2)
P: Theta(1), Q: Theta(n log n), R: Theta(n log n)

C. 6
D. 7

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following statements is false?
A. A tree with n nodes has (n - 1) edges.
B. A labeled rooted binary tree can be uniquely constructed given its postorder and preorder traversal results.
C. A complete binary tree with n internal nodes has (n + 1) leaves.
D. The maximum number of nodes in a binary tree of height h is 2^(h+1) - 1.

parsed.stem: Which of the following statements is false?

options.text: A tree with n nodes has (n - 1) edges.
A labeled rooted binary tree can be uniquely constructed given its postorder and preorder traversal results.
A complete binary tree with n internal nodes has (n + 1) leaves.
The maximum number of nodes in a binary tree of height h is 2^(h+1) - 1.

C. x(m + 1)
D. x(n + 1)

C. n(k - 1) + 1
D. n(k - 1)

sourceQuestion:
A complete n-ary tree is a tree in which each node has n children or no children. Let I be the number of internal nodes and L be the number of leaves in a complete n-ary tree. If n = 4 and I = 41, what is the value of L?

A. 121
B. 122
C. 123
D. 124

sourceQuestion:
An articulation point in a connected graph is a vertex such that removing the vertex and its incident edges disconnects the graph into two or more connected components. Let T be a tree obtained by doing DFS in a connected undirected graph G.

Which of the following options is/are correct?

A. Root of T can never be an articulation point in G.
B. A leaf of T can be an articulation point in G.
C. A leaf of T can never be an articulation point in G.
D. If u is an articulation point in G such that x is an ancestor of u in T and y is a descendant of u in T, then all paths from x to y in G must pass through u.
sourceAnswer: D. If u is an articulation point in G such that x is an ancestor of u in T and y is a descendant of u in T, then all paths from x to y in G must pass through u.

parsed.stem:
An articulation point in a connected graph is a vertex such that removing the vertex and its incident edges disconnects the graph into two or more connected components. Let T be a tree obtained by doing DFS in a connected undirected graph G.

Which of the following options is/are correct?

options.text: Root of T can never be an articulation point in G.
A leaf of T can be an articulation point in G.
A leaf of T can never be an articulation point in G.
If u is an articulation point in G such that x is an ancestor of u in T and y is a descendant of u in T, then all paths from x to y in G must pass through u.

sourceQuestion:
Aliasing in the context of programming languages refers to:
A. multiple variables having the same memory location
B. multiple variables having the same value
C. multiple variables having the same identifier
D. multiple uses of the same variable
sourceAnswer: A. multiple variables having the same memory location

parsed.stem: Aliasing in the context of programming languages refers to:

options.text: multiple variables having the same memory location
multiple variables having the same value
multiple variables having the same identifier
multiple uses of the same variable

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following two C code segments. Y and X are one and two dimensional arrays of size n and n x n respectively, where 2 <= n <= 10. Assume that in both code segments, elements of Y are initialized to 0 and each element X[i][j] is initialized to i+j. Further assume that when stored in main memory all elements of X are in the same memory page frame.

Code segment 1:
for (i=0; i<n; i++)
    Y[i] += X[0][i];

Code segment 2:
for (i=0; i<n; i++)
    Y[i] += X[i][0];

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
S1: Final contents of array Y will be identical in both code segments.
S2: Elements of X accessed inside the loop shown in code segment 1 are contiguous in main memory.
S3: Elements of X accessed inside the loop shown in code segment 2 are contiguous in main memory.

A. Only S2 is correct
B. Only S3 is correct
C. Only S1 and S2 are correct
D. Only S1 and S3 are correct
sourceAnswer: C. Only S1 and S2 are correct

options.text: Only S1 and S2 are correct
Only S1 and S3 are correct

sourceAnswer:
B. a d
   b e
   c f

options.text: a b c d e f
a d b e c f
a c e b d f
a e d c b f

sourceQuestion:
An unrestricted use of the "go to" statement is harmful because of which of the following reason(s):
A. It makes it more difficult to verify programs.
B. It makes programs more inefficient.
C. It results in the compiler generating longer programs.
D. It results in the compiler generating longer machine code.
sourceAnswer: A. It makes it more difficult to verify programs.

parsed.stem: An unrestricted use of the "go to" statement is harmful because of which of the following reason(s):

options.text: It makes it more difficult to verify programs.
It results in the compiler generating longer programs.
It results in the compiler generating longer machine code.

sourceQuestion:
An unrestricted use of the "goto" statement is harmful because
A. It makes it more difficult to verify programs.
B. It increases the running time of the programs.
C. It increases the memory required for the programs.
D. It results in the compiler generating longer machine code.
sourceAnswer: A. It makes it more difficult to verify programs.

parsed.stem: An unrestricted use of the "goto" statement is harmful because

options.text: It makes it more difficult to verify programs.
It increases the running time of the programs.
It increases the memory required for the programs.
It results in the compiler generating longer machine code.
